title: Koleksi Puisi Masturbasi
description: Koleksi Puisi Masturbasi is a poetry by Benz Ali. Banned for its suggestive name and immoral content.
descriptionParagraphs: Koleksi Puisi Masturbasi is a poetry by Benz Ali. Banned for its suggestive name and immoral content.

The surviving record is interesting because it shows how even ordinary-looking books can acquire a charged political afterlife. As a poetry, it can be read not only for subject matter but for the way form, tone, and circulation make a text feel dangerous, intimate, or politically usable to anxious officials.

It also matters as part of a wider censorship history in Malaysia. The present page is a dossier starter built from source-tracked ban records; the surviving note currently says: Banned for its suggestive name and immoral content. The home ministry said these books have been banned under Section 7(1) of Act 301 as they are considered 'undesirable publications' on January 22, 2025, and was later. More publication history, translations, and close reading can be added later.

banningParagraphs: Koleksi Puisi Masturbasi entered censorship debates as a poetry associated with controversy, publication history, and state scrutiny. In the current dossier, the main state objections cluster around political sensitivity.

The home ministry said these books have been banned under Section 7(1) of Act 301 as they are considered 'undesirable publications' on January 22, 2025, and was later publicly announced on February 8.

counterReadings.title: 100 Banned Books: Censorship Histories of World Literature
counterReadings.author: Nicholas J. Karolides, Margaret Bald, and Dawn B. Sova
counterReadings.note: A compact reference on how censorship systems moved across states, churches, and courts.

counterReadings.title: Banned Books: 387 B.C. to 1978 A.D.
counterReadings.author: Anne Lyon Haight
